query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "dragmesh-2: physically plausible dexterous hand-object interaction wit" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it investigates the same core mechanism (dexterous hand-object interaction) and domain (real-world robotic manipulation) as the user's question, specifically addressing the challenge of "physically plausible" interaction through a fine-tuning approach on soft/deformable objects. Although it does not mention the specific "DragMesh-2" framework, it provides canonical prior work on the independent variable (dexterous hand policies) and dependent variable
